title: peppery crispy korean caraway seed banchan
description: a crunchy, aromatic side dish featuring caraway seed and gochujang. great for meal prep or a quick weeknight snack.
instructions: mix gochujang, soy sauce, sesame oil, brown sugar, rice vinegar, korean chili flakes, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl.
add toasted sesame seeds and chopped scallions to the bowl and stir to combine.
in a separate bowl, whisk together cornstarch and water until smooth.
add the cornstarch mixture to the gochujang mixture and stir until a smooth paste forms.
heat a small skillet over medium-high heat and add 1-2 teaspoons of oil.
when the oil is hot, add a single layer of the caraway seed mixture to the skillet and press down to form a flat layer.
cook for 2-3 minutes, until the caraway seed mixture is crispy and golden brown.
flip the caraway seed mixture and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until crispy and golden brown.
repeat the process with the remaining caraway seed mixture, adjusting the heat as needed.
serve the crispy caraway seed banchan hot, garnished with additional toasted sesame seeds and chopped scallions if desired.
cool completely before storing in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
reheat in the microwave or oven before serving.
